description:
# Why

bl-1098 made `.balls/plugins/` a symlink into `.balls/state-repo/.balls/plugins/`
in federated mode, materialized by `state_repo::ensure`. It deliberately left
the git-hygiene side of the transition alone — this ball closes that.

When `bl remaster --commit <url>` flips a standalone repo to federated:

- `state_repo::ensure` removes the `.gitkeep`-only `.balls/plugins/` directory
  and drops a symlink in its place. But `.balls/plugins/.gitkeep` is still
  *tracked* in git, so `git status` shows it as deleted.
- The new `.balls/plugins` symlink is untracked and has no `.gitignore` entry —
  it would be committed by a careless `git add -A`, baking one user's filesystem
  layout into the shared repo.
- `.balls/state-repo/` itself is also untracked with no `.gitignore` entry. This
  one predates bl-1098 (a pre-existing gap), but it compounds the same mess.

Net effect: a freshly-federated repo has a permanently noisy `git status` until
the user manually `git rm`s the `.gitkeep` and hand-edits `.gitignore`. That is
exactly the kind of silent-footgun state `ensure_main_gitignore` exists to prevent.

# Scope

- On the federated flip (`bl remaster --commit <url>`, and the `bl init` path
  that materializes a seeded `master_url`): add `.balls/plugins` and
  `.balls/state-repo` to `.gitignore`, and `git rm --cached`
  `.balls/plugins/.gitkeep` (plus any other now-symlink-shadowed tracked content).
- On `bl remaster --detach`: reverse it — drop `.balls/plugins` from `.gitignore`
  and re-track `.balls/plugins/.gitkeep` so the standalone repo is clean again.
- Decide the seam: a federated arm on `store_init::ensure_main_gitignore`, or
  direct handling in the remaster command. ensure_main_gitignore already owns
  the non-stealth `.balls/tasks` / `.balls/worktree` entries, so it is the
  natural home — but it is currently only called at init time, not remaster time.
- Tests: federated flip leaves a clean `git status`; detach restores standalone
  shape; idempotent on re-run.

# Relation

Composes with bl-82a4 (symlink-based config symmetry), whose redirected design
makes `bl remaster` "handle whatever state it finds". This could fold into that
ball, but it is a concrete, independently-shippable hygiene fix and shouldn't
wait on bl-82a4's discuss-first design loop.

# Origin

bl-1098 close-out review (2026-05-20). Deferred scope explicitly flagged at
delivery; user asked for it to be tracked as its own ball.
title: bl remaster --commit: gitignore + untrack .balls/plugins on the federated flip
